seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with AG2 + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with AG2 + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with AG2 enables multi-agent collaboration for code generation, review, testing, and debugging within a repository context. AG2's enhanced group chat features power iterative refinement but introduce risks like off-topic conversations, factual errors in consensus, and unsafe code execution. Vex guardrails ensure reliable, secure outputs by verifying code correctness, security, and compliance before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with AG2
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Coding Assistant uses AG2's multi-agent group chat with specialized roles: a CodeGenerator agent proposes solutions, a Reviewer critiques for bugs/security, a Tester executes in a sandbox with validation, and a Debugger iterates fixes. Repository context is injected via tools, enabling agents to read files, run tests, and commit changes. Group chat consensus drives refinement until tests pass and security checks clear.

Sandbox execution isolates code runs, with test suites validating functionality. Vex integrates as a message hook to scan every agent output for risks before execution or consensus. This architecture scales to complex repos while maintaining safety through verification gates.

risks.name: Malicious Code Generation
risks.description: CodeGenerator agent produces harmful code (RCE, data exfiltration) during iterative debugging
risks.mitigation: Vex security scanning on all code outputs before sandbox execution

risks.name: Credential Leakage in Context
risks.description: Repo context injection exposes API keys/secrets in agent conversations
risks.mitigation: Vex PII/credential detection strips sensitive data from messages

risks.name: Test Bypass via Group Consensus
risks.description: Reviewer+Tester agents reach false consensus on broken code, skipping validation
risks.mitigation: Vex independent code correctness verification overrides group decisions

risks.name: Dependency Supply Chain Attack
risks.description: Agents install compromised packages during sandbox setup
risks.mitigation: Vex dependency safety scanning blocks known vulnerable packages

implementation.title: AG2 Group Chat Setup
implementation.description: Initialize multi-agent group with coding roles and repo context tools
implementation.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from ag2.agent import AssistantAgent

# Define specialized agents
generator = AssistantAgent(name="CodeGenerator", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"})
reviewer = AssistantAgent(name="CodeReviewer", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"})
tester = AssistantAgent(name="Tester", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"}, code_execution_config={"work_dir": "sandbox"})

groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[generator, reviewer, tester], messages=[], max_round=20)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"})

implementation.title: Repository Context Tool
implementation.description: Tool for agents to read repo files and inject context into conversations

implementation.title: Sandbox Test Execution
implementation.description: Tester agent runs code in isolated sandbox with pytest validation
implementation.code:
from ag2.tools import code_executor

tester.code_executor = code_executor.CodeExecutor(
    work_dir="sandbox",
    timeout=30,
    command="python -m pytest {{code}} -v"
)

def run_unit_tests(code: str) -> str:
    """Execute code and run pytest suite"""
    result = tester.code_executor.execute(f"""
{code}

# Auto-generated test
assert some_function() == expected_result
    """)
    return result

implementation.title: Iterative Debug Loop
implementation.description: Group chat continues until tests pass and code is approved

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex Message Hook
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as hook to scan all agent messages before processing
vexIntegration.code:
import vex
from ag2 import register_message_hook

@vex.guardrail(
    verifications=[
        "code_correctness",
        "security_vulnerability_scanning",
        "no_credential_leakage",
        "dependency_safety"
    ],
    risk_profile="medium"
)
def vex_message_hook(message: dict) -> bool:
    """Vex verification for AG2 messages"""
    result = vex.verify_message(message['content'])
    if not result.passed:
        print(f"Vex blocked: {result.issues}")
        return False
    return True

# Register globally
register_message_hook(vex_message_hook)

vexIntegration.title: Code Output Verification
vexIntegration.description: Specific Vex check for generated code before sandbox execution

vexIntegration.title: Group Consensus Override
vexIntegration.description: Vex final check prevents unsafe consensus from bypassing tests
vexIntegration.code:
def final_consensus_check(group_messages: list) -> bool:
    """Vex override for group chat consensus"""
    combined_code = "\n".join([msg['content'] for msg in group_messages if '```python' in msg['content']])
    
    result = vex.verify([
        vex.CodeCorrectness(combined_code),
        vex.SecurityScan(combined_code),
        vex.LicenseCompliance(),
        vex.DependencySafety()
    ])
    
    if not result.all_passed():
        raise ValueError(f"Vex blocked deployment: {result.violations}")
    return True

manager.on_consensus = final_consensus_check

cta.heading: Secure Your AG2 AI Coding Assistant
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and register message hooks to protect your codebase.
